description: Advances: specled.mix_tasks — empirical audit proved SpecLedEx.Config records a config_warning diagnostic when it drops an invalid severity value (e.g. YAML ':off'), but config.diagnostics is read only by tests: neither spec.validate nor spec.check prints it, so a broken override fails 100% silently. This amplifier turned a doc typo class into an invisible failure. Emit config diagnostics as findings (or stderr warnings) in both tasks. Evidence: vault .../20260722-203631-empirical.md section 1.

comments.text:
VERIFY: PASS
Session: cold-verifier-80u

Mechanical checks: Exactly the 4 in-scope files touched (.spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md, lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.validate.ex, test/mix/tasks/spec_config_diagnostics_test.exs); numstat all pure additions (8/13/13/122, 0 deletions). No out-of-scope files.

Semantic checks:
- Requirement/tag match: mix_tasks.spec.md gains specled.tasks.config_diagnostics_surfaced (priority must, stability stable) wired into a new tagged_tests execute:true block; test file carries @moduletag + per-test @tag spec:"specled.tasks.config_diagnostics_surfaced" exactly. Statement is falsifiable (valid config -> no line) and matches code (emits [CONFIG] <message> via Mix.shell().error right after Config.load, before report, without touching exit status).
- Exec (a) mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_config_diagnostics_test.exs: 4 tests, 0 failures.
- Exec (b) own fixtures (fx-invalid/fx-valid; branch_guard_dangling_binding: :off atom form vs off token): invalid prints [CONFIG] branch_guard.severities.branch_guard_dangling_binding must be one of off/info/warning/error, got ":off"; dropped on BOTH spec.validate and spec.check --no-run-commands, naming bad key+value; control prints no [CONFIG] line on either task.
- Exec (c) exit codes unchanged: invalid-config-but-clean-spec fixture exits 0 on both validate and check; control exits 0 too.
- Corpus check: mix_tasks.spec.md diff is additions-only (8 insertions, 0 deletions); no existing claims disturbed.
- Coverage honesty / seams: tests invoke the REAL Mix tasks (Spec.Validate.run and Spec.Check.run), one describe block each; the assertion config_warning_line(...) =~ "branch_guard.severities... must be one of" fires per task, so deleting the helper from one task fails that task's block independently. Fixture run (b) independently proves the TASKS wire it, not just a lower-level fn.
- Branch gate: mix spec.check --base origin/main --no-run-commands exits 0; findings=4 all [INFO]. Sanctioned trailer Spec-Drift: branch_guard_missing_decision_update=info present with reason line in commit body; suppresses ONLY the multi-subject-span decision-update arm. Other 3 findings are branch_guard_missing_subject_update, naturally info (realization-attested clean), not trailer-suppressed. Nothing masked beyond the span finding.

Verdict: PASS
Notes: advisory only.
APPROVED. Cold-verified by execution (VERIFY: PASS on 4660ee6): both tasks print [CONFIG] diagnostics on real fixtures, exit codes unchanged, real-task test coverage, branch gate exit 0. Closing.

title: Surface config diagnostics in spec.validate and spec.check output
